title: Identity: add X25519 HPKE keypair field + 64-byte keyfile
description:
What: Add an independent X25519 HPKE keypair to the Identity struct and update the keyfile format from 32 bytes to 64 bytes (Ed25519 seed || X25519 seed).

Algorithm:
1. Read /home/mark/WORK/nie/core/src/identity.rs
2. Add field to Identity struct (after signing_key):
   hpke_secret: x25519_dalek::StaticSecret  // separate entropy, never Debug
3. Update Identity::generate() to also generate 32 random bytes for X25519:
   let hpke_seed: [u8; 32] = rand::random();
   let hpke_secret = x25519_dalek::StaticSecret::from(hpke_seed);
4. Add Identity::from_secret_bytes to accept 64-byte input (Ed25519_seed || X25519_seed):
   Update signature: pub fn from_secret_bytes(bytes: &[u8; 64]) -> Self
   bytes[0..32] -> Ed25519 signing_key
   bytes[32..64] -> X25519 hpke_secret
5. Add methods:
   pub fn hpke_secret_bytes(&self) -> [u8; 32]  // StaticSecret::to_bytes()
   pub fn hpke_pub_key_bytes(&self) -> [u8; 32]  // x25519_dalek::PublicKey::from(&self.hpke_secret).to_bytes()
6. Update load_identity() in CLI or wherever keyfile is read:
   - Must now validate len == 64 (not 32)
   - Clear error: 'keyfile corrupt: expected 64 bytes'
7. Update save/init code to write 64-byte keyfile
8. Add runtime assertion: hpke_secret_bytes() != signing_key.to_bytes()[0..32] (key separation)
9. Run cargo check --workspace

Defensive programming rules:
- StaticSecret must NOT implement Debug (same as SigningKey)
- Never log hpke_secret_bytes() — add a comment wherever hpke_secret_bytes() is called
- key separation assertion: panic! if Ed25519 seed == X25519 seed (astronomically unlikely but required by CLAUDE.md wallet security rules)
- hpke_secret_bytes() is for keyfile storage only; clear error if called in wrong context
- from_secret_bytes now takes [u8; 64] — this is a breaking change; update all callers (load_identity, tests)
- 64-byte file: first 32 = Ed25519 seed, last 32 = X25519 seed (document in a comment)
acceptance_criteria: cargo check --workspace exits 0; Identity::generate() produces 64-byte keyfile content; from_secret_bytes accepts [u8;64]; hpke_pub_key_bytes() returns 32-byte X25519 public key; load_identity rejects 32-byte files with descriptive error
